apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of literary devices such as imagery, metaphor, and irony in the play.
Students will be able to analyze the character of Andrew Crocker-Harris and his relationships with others.
Students will be able to evaluate the dramatic structure and techniques used by the playwright.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the social context in which the play is set and its impact on the characters.
Students will be able to compare the play with other works of the same author or playwright.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of the play in conveying its themes and messages.
remember: Students will be able to recall and identify the characters, settings, and plot of the play 'The Browning Version'.
Students will be able to describe the major events and turning points in the play.
Students will be able to summarize the play in a concise manner.
understand: Students will be able to analyze the themes of the play such as isolation, loneliness, and regret.
Students will be able to explain the emotional states of the characters and how they relate to each other.
Students will be able to identify the symbolism in the play and its significance.

ncert_objectives: To understand the themes and motifs of the play and how they relate to the characters and setting.
To analyze the character of Andrew Crocker-Harris and his role in the play.
To evaluate the dramatic structure and techniques used by the playwright to convey the themes and messages.
board_exam_objectives: To analyze the character of Andrew Crocker-Harris and his relationships with others.
To evaluate the themes of the play such as isolation, loneliness, and regret.
To identify and explain the symbolism in the play and its significance.
